J28 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95gl.l_2329
t9t6) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322_0886
October 28,2009
James Johnson
Coastal Program Analyst II
California Coastal Commission
South Central Coast Area
89 South Califomia St., Suite 200
Ventura, CA 93001
Your Request for Advice
OurFile No. A-09-221
Dear N{r. Johnson:
Re:
This letter responds to your request for advice on the conflict of interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 This letter is based ,ot.ty ln tn"
facts presented; the Fair Political Practices Commission does not act as a finder of fact
when it renders advice. lln re oglesby (1975) I FPPC ops. 71.) Nothing in this letter
may be construed toevaluate any conduct that has already taken place. F"l"or"-b"u. in
mind also that our advice is limited to obligations arising'under the Act, and does not
consider other bodies of law such as common-law conflIct of interest.
QUESTTON
Do you have a conflict of interest that would disqualify you from reviewing and
preparing a staff recommendation to the coastal commission regarding its upcoming
decision on the Local Coastal Program Amendment for the city of santa Barbara?
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government code Sections g 1000 through gl0l4. All
statutory references are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices commission are contained in Sections rar ro,r,rnugi rsssz of Title 2 of the calit.rnia
code of Regulations' All regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2. Division'6 of the calitbmia Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-09-221
Page No. 2
CONCLUSION
No' Because your property lies approximately 3,000 ft. from the property that is
the subject of the decision in quesiion, thl Act p."rurn"r',t ut any foreseeable financial
effects of the decision on your economic interest ir t;;; prop"ny would not be material.
This presumption may be rebutted, however, uy proororipecific circumstances relating
to the decision, its financial effect, or the naturi of yor, ,"ul prop"rty, which make it
reasonably foreseeable that the decision would inficthave a material financial effect on
your property's value' If you believe that there are any facts or circumstances that might
rebut this legal presumption, you should consurt t.r;;g"r;y,s counser or a qualified real
estate appraiser before beginning work on this project. -J - -vE"vv' vr q Yss
FACTS
You are a coastal Program Analyst II for the california coastal cominission, a
position designated by the coistal commission's conflict of Interest code. iou may be
assigned to prepare a staff recommendation for the courta commission to assist in its
decision on the LoSl--co.astal Program Amendment ror ttre city of santa Barbara
(hereafter "the LCPA"), involvinfproperty commonly known as veronica Meadows.
As we understand your account of the facts, the LCPA proposes to apply land use
and zoning designations to a parcel annexed irto trr-ity or s*ru Barbara 1i,the city,,;,
to modify the land::"^3d zoning designationr on * uo.;acent city parcel, and ro create a
new zone district (sP-9) to establish development stand;;; for the veronica Meadows
Specific Plan, Las fos-ita1 valley. More-spicificalry, trre city seeks approval of an
adjustment to the city's LCP Map to include pro;""y'i;rh; ciry coastal plan thar was
previously under the county coaital plan. This toastat zane property wourd be
transferred to the city in the same configuration.,nptojra uy the county. city LCp
policies would remain unchanged and no textual changes tothe city,s LCp are proposed.
The city's LCP land use map and zoning implementation map would be changed
as follows: 
trv. r'sl, wvLuu uE
l' Change the LCP land use designation of ApN 047-010-016 from Residential _
one Dwelling Unit per Acre to Residential - Two Dwelling Units per Acre;
2' Add the portion of APN 047-010-016 that is locaied within the coastal Zone
boundary to the City's Local Coastal Han tvtap with a designation of
Residential-- lyo Dwelling Units per Acre, B'uffer/Stream, and pedestrian/
Equestrian Trail;
3' Include the portion of APN 047-olo-ot6 that is located within the coastal
Zone bounggl i, Component I of the City,s .ourruf zone; and
4' Designate APN 047-061'026 and portion or epr.r o+ 7-ol0-ol6that is located
within the coastal Zone boundary with a ,onin!designation of Specific plan
Number Nine and Coastal Overliy Zone (Sp_9t"S_O_f).


File No. A-09_Z2t
page No. 3
The proposal coming before the coastal commission involves a total of 4.3g
acres within the coasral zone, with residentiar.rot,i;;;;lrg from -uehiy s-,b00.
8'500 sq' ft' The remainder of the 50.5 acres is Iocated ouiside the coastal zone, and
would be dedicated primarily to op€n space and creek areas. you own and occupy your
principal residence, a home locatei appioximat"ry r,ooo ie"t from veronica Meadows.
ANALYSN
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions are designed to ensure that public
officials will "perform their duties in an impartiat manne?, free from bias caised by their
own financial interests or the financial interests .rp*r"rr who have supported them.,,
(Section 81001(b).) 
. Section 87100 prohibits_any pirlli"-rrncial from making,
participating in making, or using his or her officLip"rlii", to influence a governmental
decision in which the official hai a financial interesi, urrl"r, an exception applies.
The Commi::,:l has adopted an eighrstep analysis for deciding whether an
official has a disqualifying conflict of interest. lRegutation 1g700(b).) The general rule,
however' is that a confliciof interest exists *rrr1!1"i u puuri" official makes, participates
in making' or uses his or her official position to influenJe a govemmental decision that, at
the time the decision is made, has a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on
one or more of the officiars economic interesis. lsection azto:.)
srnps r & 2: Anr you punr,rc orrrcrar,s MaxrNc, penrrcrp.lTrNc rN M.lxrNc, on
INrr,urNcrxc ^l GovnnxMENTAL Drcrsrox?
As an employee of the coastal commission you are a . public official,, under the
Act. (section 8204g.) consequently, you.may not make, participate in making, or use
your official position to influence any iecision that *ilirra'u" a reasonably fore.seeable
material financial effect on any of your economic interests. tn preparing the staff
recommendation to the coastal commission regardinjitr urrirnutr a""iiionln the LCpA,
you will be making, part-icipating in making, ". *ilr;;Jrrri"iut position to influence a
governmental decision (See Regulations tglo2.t, til:oz.z and 1g702.3, copies enclosed.)
srup 3: Do you rrlvr a porrNrrAlly Drsguarmyrxc EcoNoMrc rNrrnrsr?
A public official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of
Section 87103 if it is reasonably foreseeable rhat the ar.irion will have a material
financial effect' distinguishable from its effect on trr. puuiiJ generally, on the official, a
member of his or her immediate family, or on any one of five enumerated economic
interests, including:
o An economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a direct or indirect
investmenr of $2,000 or more (section Aircl@);r.t. iizO:.1(a)); or in which he or
she is a director, officer, panner, trustee, emproyee,'or hords any position of
management (Section 97103(d); Regulation iSiO:. f fUll;


File No. A-09-22t
Page No. 4
a
a
An economic interest in real property in which he or she has a direct or indirect
interest of $2,000 or more lSiction tiztorG); Reguration rg703.2);
An economic interest in any source of income, incruding promised income,
aggregating $500 or more within l2 months prior to tt."o'""irion (section g7103(c);
Regulation 18703.3);
An economic interest in any source-of gifts to him or her if the gifts aggregate to $420
or more within 12 months prior to the Jecision (Section gito:("), Regulation
187O3.4);
o An economic interest in hisor her persoral finances, including those of his or her
immediate fami]1 --, this is the "personal financial effects,, rule (section g7103;
Regulation 18703.5).
You have provided no information regarding any potential economic interests
other than your home, in which we assume you each have an interest of $2,000 or more.
Accordingly, our analysis is limited to your economic interest in this real property.
srBp 4: rs Youn Econourtc lNrBnrsr Drnncrr,y on rNnmrcrly rxvolvro rN THE
GovnnxuENTAL DncrsroN? 
v. ^r'Iv
"In order to determine if a governmental decision's reasonably foreseeable
financial effect on a given ".ono-L interest is material, wsmust first determine if the
economic interest is directry invorved or indirectly ir";ir; in the governmentar
decision"'(Regulation tazo+1a).; For govemmental decisions that affect real property
interests, the standards set forth in regiatio"liiio+: ,ppri] e"gurudon 18704 (a)(2).)
Regulation 19704.2(a) states, in pertinent part:
"(a) Real property in which a pubric officiar has an economic
interest is directry invorved in a governmentar decision if any of the
following apply:
"(l) The rear property in which the officiar has an interest, or any
part of that rear property, is rocated within soo feet of the boundaries (or
the proposed boundaries) of rhe pro;ierty which is the subject of the
governmental decision.,'
Because it is located more than 500 feet from the boundaries of the project area,
ffif|jiropertv 
is indirectlv invorved in decisions;;;;s the Lcp,q, [n.turo,ron


File No. A-}9-ZZ|
Page No. 5
Srrp5: Marrnr.IuTY STANDARD
A conflict of interest arises only when the reasonably foreseeable financial effect
of a govem*"n?19:.ision on a public offi"iot', ..*o-ic interest is material.
(Regulation 18700(a)') For real i.op"rty that is not directly involved in a governmental
decision, we appry the materiarity standards orReguiaiLn 1g705.2(b):
"(b) Indirectly involved real propefty interests.
"(l) Real property, other than reasehords. The financiar effect of a
governmental decision on real property which is indirectly inroi*o in the
govemmextal decision is presumea.roi to be material. This t *r6;t*
may be rebutted by proof that there ur" ,p""iri. "ir"urrr*ces regarding
the governmental decision, its f^inancial .ir".i, *o the nature of the real
property in which the public official has an economic interest, which make
it reasonably foreseeable that the decisio, *iiirr*" a material financial
effect on the rear p-roperty in which trre puuric oinciar has an interest.
Examples of specific cirCumstances thai will be considered include, but
are not limited to, circumstances where the decision affects:
"(A) The development potential or income producing potential of the real
property in which the officiar has an economic interest;
"(B) The use of the real property in which the official has an economic
interest;
"(c) The character of the .neighborhood including, but not limited to,
substantial effects on: traffic,-view, privacy,lr,liriry of use, noise revers,
air emissions, or similar traits of tf,r n"igfr6o;;;i,
we have advised that to rebut this presumption of nonmateriality, the effect of the
governmental decision must result in a clearly airii"grirt aute arra substantial effect on
the official's real property. (see, e.g. the Beigeraaiice i"trer, No. 4_05_054.)
You have not informed us of any facts that might possibly rebut this presumption.
ultimately this is a factual determination that yo, -ur"i -'ute. If you need assistance to
ensure that the presumption will not be rebutted by th; .;i;"ts of any decisions you make,
parlicipate in making, or use your official position to innr"r"", y;J;;il;;surt your
agency's counsel or a qualified real estate appraiser.
Srrp 6: Rrasonenry FoRTsTEABLE
A "clearly distinguishable and substantial effect" on your real property would be
"reasonably foreseeable'; if at the time of the ;.;ili-th.lr'i, o .,substantial likelihood,,
that such an effect w,r occur. (Reguration 1g706(a).) wh;;", an effect of a given


File No. A-09-Z2t
Page No. 6
decision is "substantially likely" at the time of the decision depends on the particular
facts and circumstances surrounding the decision. a" "if."t 
need not be certain to be
considered reasonably foreseeable,6ut it must be more rh; a mere possiblity. (In re
Thorner (1975) I FppC Ops. 198.)
SrrPS 7 ENO 8: PUBT,TC GNXrNEr,r,Y & LNC.IT,LY REQUIRED PARTICIPATION
You have not presented any facts indicating that either the ,public generally,, or
"legally required participation" exleptions would be applicable herein.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
f*;
By: Lawrence T. Woodlock
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
